data.title: Large-cap real estate stocks with A grade EPS revisions as earnings season begins
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season gets underway, the real estate sector is entering reporting season on a relative high, as investors sought defensive, income-generating exposure amid broader market volatility.

This list highlights large cap real estate stocks screened for Seeking Alpha EPS revision Quant Grades of A- or better, offering investors a look at companies in the sector demonstrating strong earnings momentum.

The top tier of the list features three companies earning the highest A+ grade: Equinix, Inc. (EQIX), a Data Center REIT with a market cap of $104.28 billion; W. P. Carey Inc. (WPC), a Diversified REIT valued at $15.70 billion; and Weyerhaeuser Company (WY), a Timber REIT with a $17.87 billion market cap. This diversity across REIT subcategories demonstrates that strong EPS revisions span multiple real estate segments.

The remaining stocks on the list include several Retail and Health Care REITs. Regency Centers Corporation (REG) and Simon Property Group, Inc. (SPG) represent the Retail REIT space with A grades, while Ventas, Inc. (VTR) earns an A grade in the Health Care REIT category. Notably, several stocks combine high EPS revision grades with favorable Quant ratings, including Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated (JLL) and W. P. Carey Inc. (WPC), both carrying Strong Buy ratings.

  1. Equinix, Inc. (EQIX): EPS revision grade A+

  2. W. P. Carey Inc. (WPC): EPS revision grade A+

  3. Weyerhaeuser Company (WY): EPS revision grade A+

  4. Regency Centers Corporation (REG): EPS revision grade A

  5. Simon Property Group, Inc. (SPG): EPS revision grade A

  6. Ventas, Inc. (VTR): EPS revision grade A

  7. Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated (JLL): EPS revision grade A-

  8. Kimco Realty Corporation (KIM): EPS revision grade A-

  9. Welltower Inc. (WELL): EPS revision grade A-

Real Estate ETFs: (VNQ), (XLRE), (IYR), (USRT), and (HOMZ)

data.title: Nine Mile Metals launches $1M private placement for exploration
data.description:
• Nine Mile Metals (VMSXF) plans a non-brokered private placement to raise up to ~$1M.
  • The offering includes up to 7.14M units priced at $0.14 each.
  • Each unit consists of one flow-through common share and one warrant.
  • Warrants are exercisable at $0.30 for two years, with potential early expiry if share price targets are met.
  • The offering is expected to close around April 20, 2026.
  • Proceeds will fund exploration at the Bathurst Mining Camp critical minerals projects.

data.title: 3 things to look out for on Thursday
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended at all-time highs as investors priced in good news on the U.S.-Iran conflict front while parsing more bank and financial earnings.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed at the record of 7,022.93 points, ending +0.8%. The heavy-tech Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) also ended at the record of 24,016.02 points, +1.6%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI), however, finished -0.2%.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 3 basis points higher at 4.28%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.77%.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were slightly lower at $91, while Brent (CO1:COM) was at $94 per barrel.

Now, here are three things to focus on for Thursday:

  • Second quarter earnings continue, with big companies such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSM) reporting, as well as Netflix (NFLX), Abbott Laboratories (ABT), Charles Schwab (SCHW), U.S. Bancorp (USB), KeyCorp (KEY), and Citizens Financial Group (CFG), among others.
  • Market investors will also receive initial jobless claims data, which is expected to be around 215,000. In addition, the Philadelphia Fed manufacturing survey will arrive, as well as a few speeches, with New York Fed President John Williams and Fed Governor Stephen Miran.
  • The U.S. and Iran have reportedly made significant progress in talks, moving closer to a framework agreement to end the war, according to two U.S. officials. Consequently, it is also reported that both countries are considering whether to extend their current ceasefire by two weeks to allow more time for peace negotiations. Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) have stayed around $91, while Brent (CO1:COM) was at $94 per barrel. Traders will continue to assess the developments.

data.title: The York Water Company announces proposed public stock offering
data.description:
• The York Water Company (YORW) on Wednesday said it plans to launch a public offering of its common stock, with an option for underwriters to purchase additional shares.
  • The company said proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including capital investments, debt repayment and potential acquisitions.
  • YORW -2.72% after hours to $29.999.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: T3 Defense reports strong Q1: revenue hits $4.2M, stock jumps 53%
data.description:
• T3 Defense (DFNS) on Wednesday announced its unaudited preliminary Q1 2026 key metrics, with a Q1 revenue of approximately ~$4.2M, driven by defense-related orders across its subsidiaries.
  • The company ended Q1 with a strong backlog of over $12.0M, while REPs reached $12.0M across all business units.
  • Based on current traction, the company reaffirmed FY26 revenue guidance of $26M, citing strong Q1 momentum and a scalable operating platform.
  • Full financial results for Q1 2026 are expected on or about May 14, 2026.
  • T3 Defense (DFNS) stock traded at ~$0.95, up ~53.1%, in after-hours trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mid-cap real estate stocks with A grade EPS revisions as earnings season begins
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season gets underway, the real estate sector is entering reporting season on a relative high, as investors sought defensive, income-generating exposure amid broader market volatility.

This list highlights mid-cap real estate stocks that have earned an “A” grade (A+, A, or A-) for EPS revisions. The companies range in market capitalization from approximately $2 billion to over $9 billion and span various real estate subsectors including specialized REITs, retail REITs, healthcare REITs, and real estate services.

The top-ranked stocks leading the list with A+ EPS revision grades are EPR Properties (EPR), Millrose Properties, Inc. (MRP), OUTFRONT Media Inc. (OUT), and Tanger Inc. (SKT). Specialized and retail REITs dominate the highest tier of the rankings, with three of the four A+ rated companies classified as Other Specialized REITs.

The remaining stocks demonstrate diversity across real estate subsectors, with “A” grades earned by companies such as American Healthcare REIT, Inc. (AHR) and Healthcare Realty Trust Incorporated (HR) in the healthcare space. The A- grade category includes real estate services company Compass, Inc. (COMP) alongside retail-focused entries like Phillips Edison & Company, Inc. (PECO) and Urban Edge Properties (UE).

Here is the list:

  1. EPR Properties (EPR) – A+

  2. Millrose Properties, Inc. (MRP) – A+

  3. OUTFRONT Media Inc. (OUT) – A+

  4. Tanger Inc. (SKT) – A+

  5. American Healthcare REIT, Inc. (AHR) – A

  6. Curbline Properties Corp. (CURB) – A

  7. Cousins Properties Incorporated (CUZ) – A

  8. DiamondRock Hospitality Company (DRH) – A

  9. Healthcare Realty Trust Incorporated (HR) – A

  10. InvenTrust Properties Corp. (IVT) – A

  11. NNN REIT, Inc. (NNN) – A

  12. Compass, Inc. (COMP) – A-

  13. Getty Realty Corp. (GTY) – A-

  14. Phillips Edison & Company, Inc. (PECO) – A-

  15. Urban Edge Properties (UE) – A-

Real Estate ETFs: (VNQ), (XLRE), (IYR), (USRT), and (HOMZ)

data.title: Nuclear energy stocks extend gains in busy news week
data.description:
Nuclear energy stocks including Oklo (OKLO), Nuscale Power (SMR), and Energy Fuels (UUUU) extended strong gains for a third straight day on Wednesday alongside a raft of news announcements in the sector.

Among nuclear names rallying so far this week: Oklo (OKLO) up 28.9%, Nuscale Power (SMR) up 20.7%, Energy Fuels (UUUU) up 15.6%, Denison Mines (DNN) up 10.8%, Lightbridge (LTBR) up 10.3%, Uranium Energy (UEC) up 9.2%, Ur-Energy (URG) up 8.2%, NexGen Energy (NXE) up 7%, Centrus Energy (LEU) up 6.9%, Cameco (CCJ) up 3.4%, BWX Technologies (BWXT) up 3%.

Various reports said Rolls-Royce (RYCEY) (RYCEF) secured £600M (~$814M) in funds from Britain's national wealth fund to develop the U.K.'s first small modular reactors.

The U.K. government approved the development of three SMRs at the Wylfa site in North Wales; the BBC reported the three units have a total output capable of powering ~3M homes for over 60 years.

The U.S. government unveiled a sweeping initiative to bring nuclear energy technology into space called the National Initiative for American Space Nuclear Power, a joint NASA–Pentagon strategy to develop nuclear power systems capable of supporting future lunar and interplanetary missions.

The initiative, officially endorsed by NASA this week, aims to deploy a 20 kwe space reactor, code named "SR-1 Freedom," for interplanetary propulsion and surface power.

Earlier this week, Canaccord Genuity issued a bullish report on the uranium sector as a "high-beta sector [that] should be a beneficiary of the increased focus on energy security which is not being reflected in stocks.

Uranium share prices currently imply an average value of US$88/lb, compared to term prices of roughly US$90-US$93/lb and Canaccord's long-term forecast of US$110/lb.

"In light of recent volatility, we highlight names with torque and positive potential catalysts," the broker said, including NexGen Energy (NXE), Uranium Energy (UEC), Denison Mines (DNN), and Atha Energy (SASKF).

ETFs: (NLR), (URA), (URNM)

data.title: Johnson Controls considers $4.5B in divestitures - Bloomberg
data.description:
Johnson Controls (JCI) is evaluating options, including a possible sale of two businesses in its security division that could be worth as much as $4.5 billion.

Johnson Controls (JCI) is working with advisers to garner interest for its Access Control and Intrusion Detection units, according to a Bloomberg report on Monday, which cited people familiar with the matter. JCI is trying to sell the units separately, though it could decide to sell them to a single buyer.

Deliberations are at an early stage, and the company could decide to keep one or both of the divisions. Johnson Controls declined to comment to Bloomberg.

Johnson Controls (JCI)  completed the $6.9 billion sale of its Residential and Light Commercial HVAC business to Bosch Group last year. 

Shares of the industrial conglomerate edged up by 0.6% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Cineverse appoints new CFO
data.description:
• Cineverse (CNVS) has appointed Sean McCabe as chief financial officer effective April 20, 2026.
  • McCabe previously worked at Cineverse as VP and corporate controller in 2023–2024.
  • He joins from Freestar, where he led finance, accounting, and M&A functions.
  • His appointment follows Cineverse’s acquisitions of Giant Worldwide and IndiCue to expand its ad-tech footprint.

data.title: Live Nation found guilty of monopolizing ticket market, overcharging concert-goers
data.description:
A New York federal jury found Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) overcharged customers and illegally tied the use of its company-owned venues to its concert promotion services, leaving the ticket platform liable for at least $700M in penalties. The company could also be forced to sell its Ticketmaster subsidiary.

After a six-week trial that included “dozens of witnesses,” Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) was found guilty of monopolizing the market for ticket services, concert ticketing, venues, and promotions.

According to the lawsuit that was filed by a coalition of 33 states and Washington D.C., Live Nation (LYV) engaged in “anticompetitive conduct” by overcharging customers $1.72 per ticket, leaving artists with fewer touring options, and coercing venues to use Ticketmaster over its competitors.

The Department of Justice was not part of this lawsuit as the agency previously settled with Live Nation (LYV) in March after meeting with company executives. The settlement required Ticketmaster to divest up to 13 venues, reserve 50% of its tickets for non-exclusive venues, and cap ticketing service fees at 15%.

Live Nation (LYV) has denied any wrongdoing.

While Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) shares closed more than 6% lower, rivals StubHub (STUB) and Vivid Seats (SEAT) closed 3.5% and 9.3% higher, respectively.
